messages.content:
AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi, I'm at the QuickStop gas station on 5th and Maple. There's like, two guys outside, they're yelling and one just pulled out a gun!
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you inside the gas station right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I'm behind the counter. Oh man, they're still out there, I can see them through the window. One's got a black hoodie, the other's wearing a red cap. The one with the gun—he's pointing it at the other guy!
AGENT: Is anyone else in the store with you?
USER: No, no, just me. I locked the door when I saw them, but I don’t know if they saw me. What do I do? Should I call the cops? Wait, I *am* calling the cops—
AGENT: You're doing the right thing. Stay down behind the counter and keep the phone line open. Are the suspects still outside?
USER: Yeah, yeah, they're still there. The one with the gun just shoved the other guy. Oh God, is someone coming? They’re gonna shoot!
AGENT: Units are on their way. Stay out of sight and don’t make any sudden moves. Can you still see them?
USER: I—I think they’re moving toward the side of the building. I can’t see them anymore. What if they come in here?
AGENT: Keep the door locked and stay low. Officers should be arriving any moment. Do you hear sirens yet?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I hear them! Oh thank God. I think they’re pulling up now.
AGENT: Good. Stay where you are until the officers give you the all-clear. I’ll stay on the line until they’re inside.
USER: Okay, okay. They’re getting out of their cars. One’s going around the back—
AGENT: That’s great. Just stay put and follow their instructions when they come in.
USER: Alright, they’re at the door now. I gotta go let them in.
AGENT: Understood. You did great. I’m going to end the call now so you can focus on the officers. Stay safe.
Scores: anxiety=9, severity=9, coherence=6, seriousness=10

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, hi, yeah, I'm at 452 Maple Street, in the apartment complex? There's, like, this huge storm outside and the wind is just—it's crazy, I think a tree just fell on the building next to us!
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you inside right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I'm inside, but the windows are shaking, and I heard a loud crash a second ago. I don't know if it hit our building or not. Should I go check?
AGENT: No, don't go outside. Is anyone with you in the apartment?
USER: No, it's just me. My roommate is at work. Oh man, the power just went out too!
AGENT: Alright, listen to me. Stay away from the windows. Do you have a safe place inside, like a bathroom or a closet?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I have a closet in the hallway. Should I go in there?
AGENT: Yes, go there now and stay put. Keep your phone with you. Are there any injuries you can see from where you are?
USER: I don't think so. I can't see outside from here, but I don't hear anyone screaming or anything. The wind is still really loud, though.
AGENT: Okay, help is on the way. Stay in the closet until the storm passes or until emergency crews arrive. Do you have any flashlights or candles nearby?
USER: Yeah, I have a flashlight in the kitchen. Should I grab it?
AGENT: Yes, but be quick and careful. Don't use candles—just the flashlight. Can you still hear me?
USER: Yeah, I'm back in the closet. The flashlight is here. What if the building starts to collapse?
AGENT: Try to stay calm. Emergency crews are on their way, and they'll assess the building when they arrive. For now, just stay put and keep your phone on. I'll stay on the line with you until help arrives.
USER: Okay, okay. Thank you. I'm really scared.
AGENT: I understand. You're doing great. Just focus on staying safe. Can you hear any sirens yet?
USER: Yeah, I think I hear them now. They're getting louder.
AGENT: Good. That means help is almost there. Stay where you are until they come to you.
USER: Okay, I will. Thank you so much.
AGENT: You're welcome. Stay safe.
Scores: anxiety=8, severity=7, coherence=7, seriousness=9

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Oh my God, oh my God—there's fire everywhere! I'm at the old apartment building on Maple Street, the one near the gas station. The whole first floor is engulfed!
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you inside the building right now?
USER: No, no—I'm outside, but my neighbor, Mrs. Henderson, she's still in there! She's on the second floor, and the smoke is so thick I can't even see her window anymore!
AGENT: Is anyone else still inside that you know of?
USER: I—I don't think so. Most people got out, but Mrs. Henderson has trouble walking, and I saw her go back in to get her cat. The fire trucks aren't here yet, and the flames are spreading so fast!
AGENT: I’ve got units on the way. How large is the fire right now?
USER: It started in the kitchen, but now it's covering the whole front of the building. The windows on the first floor are all broken, and the fire is climbing up the walls. The smoke is black and billowing out everywhere!
AGENT: Do not go back inside. The fire department is less than two minutes away. Is there a safe place where you can meet them when they arrive?
USER: Yes, yes—I'm across the street at the convenience store parking lot. But what about Mrs. Henderson? She can't get out on her own!
AGENT: The firefighters will handle the rescue. Stay where you are and point them toward her apartment. Can you see if there are any other hazards, like downed power lines or gas leaks?
USER: I—I don’t see any power lines, but the gas station is right next door. If the fire spreads—oh God, the whole block could go up!
AGENT: I’ve alerted the fire department about the gas station. They’ll take precautions. Stay back and keep others away from the area. Are there any injuries you can see?
USER: A few people have burns, but they’re okay. Mrs. Henderson is the only one still inside. Please hurry!
AGENT: They’re almost there. Stay on the line with me until you see them arrive. What’s your name?
USER: It's Jake. Jake Reynolds. I can hear the sirens now—they’re close!
AGENT: Good. When they arrive, tell them exactly where Mrs. Henderson is. I’ll stay on the line until you confirm they’re on scene.
USER: Okay, okay—they’re here! The fire trucks just pulled up. Thank you, thank you so much!
AGENT: You did great, Jake. Stay safe and let the firefighters do their job.
Scores: anxiety=9, severity=9, coherence=6, seriousness=10

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, hi, yeah, I think there's been a car accident. I'm on the highway, like, maybe exit 42? I'm not sure, I'm not from here.
AGENT: Okay, stay calm. Are you in a safe location right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I pulled over. There's a car flipped over, and I think someone's inside. Oh god, there's smoke or something coming out.
AGENT: Is the smoke thick? Can you see if the person inside is moving?
USER: I don't know, I don't want to get too close. The smoke isn't too bad, but I can see the driver. They're not moving. Should I do something?
AGENT: No, don't approach the vehicle. Help is on the way. Are there any other cars involved?
USER: No, just the one. It looks like it hit the guardrail and flipped. There's glass everywhere.
AGENT: Got it. Can you see any flames or is the smoke getting worse?
USER: No flames, just a little smoke. I think it's from the engine. The driver still isn't moving. How long until someone gets here?
AGENT: Emergency crews are on their way. Stay where you are and keep an eye on the situation. If the smoke gets worse or you see flames, move back further.
USER: Okay, okay. I see lights now, I think they're here. Should I stay?
AGENT: Yes, stay where you are. The responders will need to talk to you. I'll stay on the line until they arrive.
USER: Alright, they're here now. Thanks for your help.
AGENT: You did great. Stay safe and let the responders take over.
Scores: anxiety=7, severity=8, coherence=8, seriousness=9

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of the emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi. I'm at, like, 123 Maple Street. There's a, um, a giant purple elephant in my backyard, and it's, like, eating my trampoline.
AGENT: A purple elephant? Can you tell me if anyone is hurt or in danger right now?
USER: No, no, nobody's hurt. It's just, like, really weird. It's got a top hat on and everything. Should I, like, call animal control or something?
AGENT: Is the elephant aggressive or just eating the trampoline?
USER: Nah, it's just chillin'. It's, like, munching away. I think it's high or something. Should I offer it some peanuts?
AGENT: I need you to stay inside and away from the elephant. Are there any children or pets outside with it?
USER: Nope, just me and my roommate. We're, like, filming it for TikTok. This is gonna go viral!
AGENT: Sir, this is an emergency line. I need you to stop filming and stay inside. I'm sending an officer to check the situation, but this is not a joke.
USER: Oh, come on, don't be such a buzzkill. It's just a prank. My buddy dressed up as an elephant. You should see your face right now—well, not literally, but you get it.
AGENT: This is a misuse of emergency services. I'm documenting this call, and you could face legal consequences. Do not call 9-1-1 unless it's a real emergency.
USER: Alright, alright, no need to get all serious. We're done here. Later, 9-1-1 lady!
Scores: anxiety=2, severity=1, coherence=8, seriousness=0

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of the emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi. I'm at, like, the big gas station on the corner of Maple and 5th. There's this guy here, and he's, um... he's just standing there with a banana, and I think it's a gun!
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Is this person threatening anyone with the banana?
USER: Well, no, not really. He's just pointing it at people and yelling, 'Stick 'em up!' But it's definitely a banana. Like, a really yellow one.
AGENT: Are you inside the gas station right now?
USER: Yeah, I'm behind the counter. The guy's outside, but he keeps looking in. Should I call the cops or something?
AGENT: Sir, we are the police. I need you to stay where you are and keep away from the windows. Is anyone else in the store with you?
USER: Nope, just me. Oh wait, he's coming in! He's got the banana up—oh, he's just asking for a pack of gum. False alarm, I guess!
AGENT: I see. Sir, this is a serious misuse of emergency services. Officers will still respond to ensure everything is okay, but prank calls can delay help for real emergencies. Do you understand?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I get it. Sorry about that. It just looked kinda funny, you know?
AGENT: I understand, but please only call 9-1-1 for real emergencies in the future. Officers are on their way. Stay inside until they arrive.
Scores: anxiety=3, severity=1, coherence=8, seriousness=0

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi. I'm at, like, the big park downtown? The one with the fountain?
AGENT: Okay, which park specifically? There are a few downtown. And what's happening there?
USER: Oh, uh, the one with the ducks. There's, like, a guy here who's, um... he's got a gun? Or maybe it's a toy? I don't know, it looks real.
AGENT: Are you seeing this person right now? Can you describe them?
USER: Yeah, he's like, wearing a black hoodie and he's just standing there pointing it at the sky. Wait, no, now he's pointing it at the ducks. Oh man, this is so funny.
AGENT: This doesn't sound like a joke. Are you saying someone has a weapon in a public park?
USER: I mean, it's probably a water gun or something. He's laughing really hard. Oh, he just sprayed a duck! The duck is fine, though. This is hilarious.
AGENT: I need you to take this seriously. Is anyone in danger right now?
USER: Nah, the ducks are fine. The guy's just messing around. Oh, he's putting it away now. He's walking toward me. He's my friend, actually. We were just pranking.
AGENT: This is a serious misuse of emergency services. I'm going to have to send an officer to speak with you about this. What's your name?
USER: Oh, come on, it was just a joke! We didn't mean any harm. Can't you take a joke?
AGENT: This isn't a laughing matter. Prank calls tie up emergency lines and can delay response to real emergencies. An officer will be there shortly to address this.
USER: Ugh, fine. Whatever. Can I go now?
AGENT: Stay where you are. The officer will need to speak with you directly.
Scores: anxiety=2, severity=1, coherence=8, seriousness=0

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi, I think there's been a car accident. I'm on the highway, like, I-85 northbound, just past exit 33. There's a blue sedan flipped over in the median.
AGENT: Okay, I've got that location. Are you safe where you are right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I pulled over. I'm in my car. But there's smoke coming from the engine of the other car, and I don't see anyone moving inside.
AGENT: Is anyone else stopped to help?
USER: No, not yet. I just got here. Oh God, I think I see someone in the driver's seat, but they're not moving at all.
AGENT: Alright, I need you to stay where you are. Don't approach the vehicle, especially with that smoke. Are you able to see if the person inside is breathing?
USER: I—I don't know. It's hard to tell from here. The car's on its side, and the windows are all smashed. Should I go closer?
AGENT: No, stay back. The car could be unstable or catch fire. Help is on the way. Can you tell me if there are any other vehicles involved?
USER: No, just the one car. It looks like it swerved off the road and flipped. There's debris everywhere.
AGENT: Okay, I've got units responding. They should be there in about 5 minutes. Is the smoke getting worse?
USER: Yeah, it's thicker now. I can smell it from here. What if the car explodes?
AGENT: Try to stay calm. Move further away if you feel unsafe, but keep an eye on the scene so you can guide the responders when they arrive. Can you do that for me?
USER: Okay, yeah, I can do that. I'm moving back now. Oh, wait—I see lights! The fire truck is here!
AGENT: Good. Stay where you are and let them handle it. You've done great. Is there anything else you need right now?
USER: No, I think I'm okay. Thank you so much.
AGENT: You're welcome. Take care, and drive safely.
Scores: anxiety=8, severity=9, coherence=7, seriousness=9

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, hi, yeah, I'm at 452 Maple Street, and there's like, this huge tornado just touched down near the park! It's tearing up everything!
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you in a safe location right now?
USER: I—I think so? I'm in my basement, but I can hear stuff crashing outside! My neighbors, the Johnsons, they were outside just a minute ago! I don't know if they made it inside!
AGENT: Are there any injuries you can see from where you are?
USER: I can't see much, but I heard screaming earlier! Oh God, what if someone's hurt out there?
AGENT: Help is on the way. Do you know if anyone else is with you in the basement?
USER: No, it's just me. My husband is at work, and the kids are at school. I—I don't know if the schools are okay!
AGENT: Stay where you are. Keep away from windows and doors. Can you hear the tornado now?
USER: It sounds like it's moving away, but I can still hear debris hitting the house. It's so loud!
AGENT: Units are en route to your location. Do not go outside until you're told it's safe. Can you stay on the line with me?
USER: Yes, yes, I'll stay here. Please hurry!
AGENT: They're almost there. Just hold tight. I'll stay with you until help arrives.
Scores: anxiety=9, severity=9, coherence=6, seriousness=10

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi. I'm at, like, 123 Maple Street? Or wait, no—maybe it's Oak? I don't know, man, I'm not from here.
AGENT: Okay, let's start with what's happening. Are you in danger right now?
USER: Dude, it's wild. There's, like, a guy in a clown mask just chillin' in my backyard, and he's got a chainsaw. It's totally not running, though—he's just holding it. Super weird, right?
AGENT: A person in a clown mask with a chainsaw? Is this person threatening you or anyone else?
USER: Nah, he's just standing there. Like, he waved at me. I think he's lost or something. Maybe he's looking for a party?
AGENT: Have you had any interaction with this person beyond seeing them?
USER: Yeah, I yelled out the window, 'Hey, buddy, you good?' And he goes, 'Just admiring the foliage.' Then he sat down on my lawn chair. It's, like, 2 AM.
AGENT: I see. Are there any other people around, or is it just you and this individual?
USER: Just me and Clown Boy. My roommate's out of town, so it's just me. I'm kinda freaked, but also, like, is this a prank? Are you guys testing me or something?
AGENT: This doesn't sound like a test. I'm going to send an officer to check on the situation. Can you confirm the address one more time?
USER: Oh, uh, yeah. It's 123 Maple Street. Apartment 2B. But, like, don't send the cops if this is a joke, okay? I don't wanna look dumb.
AGENT: We take all calls seriously. Stay inside, lock your doors, and keep away from the windows until the officer arrives. Can you do that?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I got it. But if this is a prank, I'm gonna be so mad. You guys are gonna owe me, like, pizza or something.
AGENT: An officer will be there shortly to assess the situation. Stay on the line if you'd like, but I need to take another call now. Please stay safe.
USER: Alright, cool. Thanks, I guess. If I get arrested for this, I'm blaming you.
Scores: anxiety=3, severity=2, coherence=8, seriousness=1

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi, I think there's been a car accident. I'm on the highway, like, right near the exit for Maple Street. There's a blue car flipped over and another one just... just sitting there.
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you in a safe location right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I pulled over. I'm like a little ways back. But there's smoke coming from the blue car, and I don't see anyone moving. Oh God, what if they're hurt?
AGENT: You're doing great. Is the smoke getting worse, or is it staying about the same?
USER: It's... it's kinda thick, but it's not like, spreading or anything. I don't see flames, but it's definitely smoke. Should I go check on them?
AGENT: No, don't approach the vehicles. It's important you stay where you are. Are there any other bystanders nearby who might be able to help?
USER: No, no one else has stopped yet. It just happened like two minutes ago. I called right away. There's a guy in the other car, the one that's not flipped. He's just sitting there, kinda staring. I don't think he's okay.
AGENT: Is he moving at all? Can you tell if he's conscious?
USER: He's... he's kinda slumped over the wheel. I can't tell if he's breathing. Oh man, this is bad. What do I do?
AGENT: Help is already on the way. I need you to keep an eye on the situation, but don't put yourself in danger. Can you see any license plates or landmarks that might help us find you faster?
USER: Uh, yeah, the exit sign says Maple Street, mile marker 112. The blue car is a, uh, Honda Civic, I think. The other one is a black SUV, but I can't see the plate from here.
AGENT: That's perfect. You're being very helpful. Are there any sounds coming from the vehicles? Like horns, alarms, or anything?
USER: No, nothing like that. Just the smoke. Wait, the guy in the SUV just opened his door. He's getting out. He's stumbling a little, but he's moving.
AGENT: Okay, that's good. Tell him to move away from the vehicles and stay clear of the smoke. Can you do that?
USER: Yeah, I'll try. HEY! SIR! GET AWAY FROM THE CAR! STAY BACK!
AGENT: Good. Now, I need you to stay on the line with me until help arrives. Can you see any emergency vehicles approaching yet?
USER: No, not yet. How long do you think it'll be? The smoke is still there, and I'm worried about the guy in the Civic.
AGENT: They're on their way. You're doing everything right. Just keep the man from the SUV away from the vehicles, and stay where you are.
USER: Okay, okay. I see lights now. Yeah, that's an ambulance and a fire truck. They're pulling up now.
AGENT: Great. You can hang up now. They'll take over from here. You've been a huge help.
USER: Alright, thank you. I just hope everyone's okay.
Scores: anxiety=8, severity=8, coherence=7, seriousness=9

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of the emergency?
USER: Oh my God, oh my God—there's fire everywhere! I'm at the old apartment building on Maple and 5th, the one with the broken sign! The whole first floor is engulfed, and I can see flames shooting out the windows!
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you inside the building right now?
USER: No, no—I'm across the street, but I can see people still inside! There's a woman screaming from the second floor, and the smoke is so thick I can barely see her! The fire is spreading fast—it’s jumping to the neighboring units!
AGENT: Are there any fire escapes or exits visible from where you are?
USER: I—I don’t know! The front is blocked by flames, but maybe the back? Oh God, the woman just stopped screaming—what if she passed out?!
AGENT: I’ve already dispatched firefighters, and they’re on their way. Do not attempt to go back inside—smoke inhalation can be deadly. Can you see if there are any other people outside?
USER: There’s a few people running out now, but I don’t know if everyone’s out! The fire alarms aren’t even going off! The whole building is going to collapse—it’s an old wooden structure!
AGENT: I need you to move further back from the building. The heat and debris could be dangerous. Are there any children or elderly people you’ve seen?
USER: I—I don’t know! There’s so much smoke! The fire trucks better hurry—this is going to be a disaster!
AGENT: They’re less than two minutes away. Stay where you are and keep an eye out for anyone else escaping. Do you see any gas lines or propane tanks near the building?
USER: No, no gas lines, but there’s a car parked right next to the building that’s starting to catch fire! The whole block is in danger!
AGENT: Understood. Keep yourself safe and let the firefighters handle it. They’ll be there any second now.
USER: Okay, okay—I hear the sirens! Thank God!
AGENT: Good. Stay on the line with me until they arrive. Can you confirm if the woman from the second floor made it out?
USER: I—I don’t see her! Oh no, the roof is starting to cave in!
AGENT: Firefighters are pulling up now. I’m going to stay on the line until they take over. Just keep yourself safe.
USER: They’re here! They’re here! Thank you, thank you!
AGENT: You did great. Stay back and let them work. I’m going to end the call now—help is there.
Scores: anxiety=9, severity=10, coherence=6, seriousness=10

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of the emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi, I'm at 452 Maple Street, and there's like, a huge tree that just fell on the house next door! I think people are inside!
AGENT: Okay, stay calm. Are you safe where you are right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I'm fine, but I heard screaming from the house! The wind was crazy, and then—BAM! The tree just crashed right through the roof!
AGENT: How many people do you think might be inside?
USER: I—I don’t know! Maybe a family? I saw kids playing in the yard earlier. Oh God, what if they’re hurt?
AGENT: I’ve got help on the way. Can you see if anyone is outside or trying to get out?
USER: No, no one’s come out yet. The front door is blocked by the tree. I—I don’t know what to do!
AGENT: You’re doing great. Stay back from the house—don’t try to go in yourself. The fire department will be there soon to help.
USER: Okay, okay. Hurry, please!
AGENT: They’re almost there. Just stay on the line with me until they arrive, alright?
USER: Yeah, I—I will. Thank you.
Scores: anxiety=8, severity=9, coherence=7, seriousness=10

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi, I'm at the QuickMart on 5th and Maple. There's like, two guys just robbed the place and they're running out now with guns!
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you inside the store right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I'm behind the counter. Oh man, they just shot at the clerk! He's on the floor, I don't know if he's okay!
AGENT: Is the clerk conscious? Can you check if he's breathing?
USER: I—I don't know, I'm scared! They're still outside, I can see them through the window. One's got a black hoodie, the other's wearing a red cap!
AGENT: Listen to me carefully. I need you to stay down and away from the windows. Police are on their way. Are the suspects still in sight?
USER: No, no, they just got into a blue sedan, like an older model, and they're speeding off down Maple!
AGENT: Did you see which way they turned after leaving the parking lot?
USER: Uh, left! They turned left onto Maple, going east! Please hurry, the clerk isn't moving!
AGENT: Units are two minutes out. Stay on the line with me. Is there anyone else injured in the store?
USER: No, no, just the clerk. I think he's bleeding bad. Oh God, what if he dies?
AGENT: Help is almost there. Keep pressure on any wounds if you can do so safely. Can you see the police arriving yet?
USER: Yeah! Yeah, I see them pulling up now! Thank you, thank you so much!
AGENT: Stay where you are until the officers give you the all-clear. They'll need to speak with you. You did great.
Scores: anxiety=9, severity=10, coherence=6, seriousness=10

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of the emergency?
USER: Uh, hi, yeah, I think there's been a car accident. I'm on the highway, like, right near the exit for Maple Street. There's a blue car flipped over and another one just... just sitting there.
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you in a safe location right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I pulled over. I'm on the shoulder. But there's smoke coming from the blue car, and I don't see anyone moving. Oh God, what if they're hurt?
AGENT: I understand this is scary. Can you see if anyone is getting out of the other car?
USER: The other car? Uh, yeah, a guy just got out. He's walking around like he's okay, but the blue car... no one's moving. The smoke is getting worse!
AGENT: Is the smoke thick or just a little?
USER: It's pretty thick. I can see flames now. Oh no, the guy from the other car is running toward it! Should he do that?
AGENT: No, tell him to get back! He shouldn't approach. Are there any other bystanders?
USER: I don't see anyone else. Just me and him. He's yelling at the car now. I think there's someone inside!
AGENT: I've got help on the way. Keep everyone back from the car. The fire could spread or the car could explode.
USER: Okay, okay. I'll tell him. Hurry, please!
AGENT: Help is on the way. Stay on the line with me until they arrive. What's your name?
USER: It's Sarah. I'm staying back. The guy is still yelling, but he's not going closer. The flames are bigger now.
AGENT: Sarah, you're doing great. Keep everyone at a safe distance. The fire trucks and ambulances are almost there.
Scores: anxiety=8, severity=9, coherence=7, seriousness=10

AGENT: 9-1-1, what's the address of your emergency?
USER: Uh, yeah, hi—I think there’s been a car accident. I’m on the highway, like, right near the exit for Maplewood. There’s a blue sedan flipped over in the median.
AGENT: Okay, I need you to stay calm. Are you in a safe spot right now?
USER: Yeah, yeah, I pulled over. I’m in my car. But there’s smoke coming from the engine of the other car—I don’t know if it’s gonna catch fire or something.
AGENT: Is anyone still inside the flipped vehicle?
USER: I—I can’t tell. The windows are all smashed. There’s a person lying on the ground next to it, though. They’re not moving.
AGENT: Are they breathing? Can you see their chest rising?
USER: I don’t know, I’m too far away. Should I go check? What if the car explodes?
AGENT: No, stay where you are. Help is on the way. Can you see any other injuries or hazards?
USER: There’s gas leaking, I think. And another car just swerved to avoid the wreck—oh God, they almost hit me too!
AGENT: I need you to focus. Are there any other people involved in the crash?
USER: No, just the one car. But the person on the ground—are they gonna be okay? I don’t see them moving at all.
AGENT: Emergency crews are almost there. Stay on the line with me. Can you see any flames or is the smoke getting worse?
USER: The smoke’s thicker now. I think I hear sirens—yeah, I see lights coming!
